title: daphne laureola subsp. latifolia
intro: Daphne laureola subsp. latifolia, also known as the spurge laurel, is a shrub species in the family Thymelaeaceae, native to Europe. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Daphne laureola subsp. latifolia include its ability to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems, making it a natural remedy for colds and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions or taken internally to boost the immune system.
